title: Benchmarking in Sandboxes
concept: Running evaluation suites in isolated environments to prevent agents from polluting production logs.

exercises.prompt: What is an important reason for using benchmarks in evaluating AI agents?

options.text: To ensure all AI agents are evaluated the same way
To test agents in safe, isolated environments
To increase the AI agents' ability to pollute production logs

exercises.explain: The correct answer highlights the importance of testing in safe environments to avoid negative impacts on real-world data.

cards.title: What Are Sandboxes?
cards.body: Sandboxes are safe spaces where we can test our AI agents without affecting real systems.

cards.title: Benchmarking
cards.body: Benchmarking is measuring how well an AI agent performs specific tasks compared to standards or other agents.

cards.title: Like a Science Lab
cards.body: Running evaluations in a sandbox is like conducting chemistry experiments in a lab. It keeps dangerous reactions contained!

cards.title: Sandbox Testing
cards.body: Imagine testing a new robot in a virtual house. If it breaks something, it won’t cause real damage!

cards.title: Myth: Sandboxes Are Just Extra Work
cards.body: Some think sandboxes are unnecessary, but they help catch mistakes before they affect real users!

cards.title: Safe vs. Unsafe Testing
cards.body: Using sandboxes avoids real-world problems while testing. It’s a controlled way to evaluate performance.

exercises.prompt: Why should AI agents be evaluated in sandboxes?

options.text: To save time by skipping evaluations
To prevent any harm to live data
To increase the amount of data to record
To allow more errors in production

exercises.explain: Evaluating in sandboxes keeps production data safe by preventing harmful changes while testing.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Using a sandbox can prevent agents from polluting production logs.
exercises.explain: True! Sandboxes are designed to isolate tests and protect live environments.

exercises.prompt: A sandbox is a safe __________ for testing AI agents.
exercises.sentence: A sandbox is a safe environment for testing AI agents.
exercises.explain: The correct word 'environment' describes the safe space where testing takes place.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes a benefit of sandbox testing?

options.text: Sandboxes prevent testing mistakes from affecting users.
Sandboxes limit the number of agent evaluations.

exercises.explain: The correct option highlights how sandboxes offer protection against impacts on users during evaluations.

exercises.prompt: What is one major reason for using sandboxes when benchmarking AI agents?

options.text: To ensure benchmarking affects production data.
To isolate tests and protect real-world systems.
To avoid using evaluation suites altogether.
To increase the risk of agent errors in testing.

exercises.explain: Isolating tests in sandboxes prevents harmful impacts on real data, making benchmarking more reliable.
